title: The one-cell midpoint rule is additive

source.citation: Alfio Quarteroni, Riccardo Sacco, and Fausto Saleri, Numerical Mathematics, 2nd ed., Springer, 2007, Ch. 9 (Numerical Integration).
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The midpoint one-cell quadrature rule is additive in the sampled value.
assumptions: f and g are midpoint samples

declarations.name: midpoint1
declarations.statement: def midpoint1 (h fm : Real) : Real := h * fm

declarations.name: midpoint1_add
declarations.statement: theorem midpoint1_add (h f g : Real) : midpoint1 h (f + g) = midpoint1 h f + midpoint1 h g

tasks.input: Translate the quadrature theorem `midpoint1_add` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem midpoint1_add (h f g : Real) : midpoint1 h (f + g) = midpoint1 h f + midpoint1 h g

tasks.input: Identify the main algebraic or order lemma needed for the quadrature proof.
